item.title: if you have a town full of animals and not one good-time jamboree jug band, then i don't know what to tell you
item.content:encoded: It was… fine? There weren't any major problems and it seems like this was executed with a fine level of technical skill. But there isn't much there there. I didn't get the sense of anything meaningful or worth caring about, just a bunch of people turned into animals; the author commentary makes it seem like there was an attempt to split the difference between profound and goofy, with the end result that it's too shallow for the former and too lengthy for the latter. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP uses the Anomaly Classification System, an unofficial system created by some users recently. The explanation of what all the classes mean can be found here: http://www.scp-wiki.net/anomaly-classification-system-guide

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can you explain the Disruption Class scale to me? I'm familiar with the Threat Level and equivalents, but the way this is split into two subclassifications after the main class confuses me overall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was thinking Euclid because of the same reason you can’t just throw most humanoids into a box. More -A instances will form. Things fly, dig, maybe just squeeze through the fence. The -A have also shown to be against their containment, destroying drones and such. We can’t just ditch it behind a fence. If I get more questions regarding the object class, I might change it, but I think the Euclid classification is justified.

Anyways, the feedback is really appreciated. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting concept, interesting imagery, but it just didn't do anything for me. Novote.

Side note, why is this Euclid? It just seems like preventing access is all that's needed to prevent additional -A's from forming,

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
and it does a very good job.

glad you liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: im so confused but I’m 1+ anyway because like…. tree work at cash register……….

item.title: To the place I belong.
item.content:encoded:
Required Listening

Take me home, country roads. A nice little story about some animals just wanting to belong. I’ve left the ending up to interpretation, so you can either choose the more meaningful ending, or the dumb pun ending I was originally going to make. Thanks to my fabulous critters

  • T Rutherford
  • Laneous
  • LukeLukens
  • Jiwoahn

for their amazing suggestions and support. And may we find a place where we belong. Probably West Virginia.

Other Articles of Mine
